% AUTO-GENERATED from build/hover-definitions.yaml — do not edit
% Regenerate: python3 build/generate-hover.py
\usepackage{etoolbox}

\csdef{hover@two-dimensional electron gas}{A thin layer where electrons can only move in two directions — trapped on a sheet. Found inside every computer chip and in Earth's magnetic field. Different physics applies in two dimensions than in three.}
\csdef{hover@Two-dimensional electron gas}{A thin layer where electrons can only move in two directions — trapped on a sheet. Found inside every computer chip and in Earth's magnetic field. Different physics applies in two dimensions than in three.}
\csdef{hover@topological order}{A property where information is stored in global patterns, not in any single place. Extremely hard to destroy — you would have to disrupt the entire pattern at once, not just a piece of it.}
\csdef{hover@Topological order}{A property where information is stored in global patterns, not in any single place. Extremely hard to destroy — you would have to disrupt the entire pattern at once, not just a piece of it.}
\csdef{hover@entanglement}{Two particles linked so that measuring one instantly reveals information about the other, no matter how far apart. Einstein called it 'spooky action at a distance.' Demonstrated in labs worldwide since the 1970s.}
\csdef{hover@Entanglement}{Two particles linked so that measuring one instantly reveals information about the other, no matter how far apart. Einstein called it 'spooky action at a distance.' Demonstrated in labs worldwide since the 1970s.}
\csdef{hover@quantum teleportation}{Transferring quantum information from one place to another without it crossing the space between. Not science fiction — demonstrated in labs since 1997. Requires entanglement plus an ordinary communication channel.}
\csdef{hover@Quantum teleportation}{Transferring quantum information from one place to another without it crossing the space between. Not science fiction — demonstrated in labs since 1997. Requires entanglement plus an ordinary communication channel.}
\csdef{hover@nonlocal}{Connected across distance without anything traveling between. In quantum systems, distant points share correlations — information about one place is encoded in another. This is a measured fact, not speculation.}
\csdef{hover@Nonlocal}{Connected across distance without anything traveling between. In quantum systems, distant points share correlations — information about one place is encoded in another. This is a measured fact, not speculation.}
\csdef{hover@autocatalytic}{A network of processes where each process's outputs help sustain other processes in the set. The result is a self-maintaining cycle — a hallmark of living systems. The math is substrate-independent: it works in chemical reactions (origin of life) and in quantum substrates (the Flat).}
\csdef{hover@Autocatalytic}{A network of processes where each process's outputs help sustain other processes in the set. The result is a self-maintaining cycle — a hallmark of living systems. The math is substrate-independent: it works in chemical reactions (origin of life) and in quantum substrates (the Flat).}
\csdef{hover@classical backchannel}{An ordinary communication channel — radio, light, electrical signal. Required to complete quantum teleportation, which is why teleportation cannot transmit information faster than light.}
\csdef{hover@Classical backchannel}{An ordinary communication channel — radio, light, electrical signal. Required to complete quantum teleportation, which is why teleportation cannot transmit information faster than light.}
\csdef{hover@magnetosphere}{Earth's magnetic field, extending thousands of kilometers into space. Contains thin layers where charged particles are trapped flat — two-dimensional electron gases on a planetary scale.}
\csdef{hover@Magnetosphere}{Earth's magnetic field, extending thousands of kilometers into space. Contains thin layers where charged particles are trapped flat — two-dimensional electron gases on a planetary scale.}
\csdef{hover@guided deduction}{Teaching someone to discover the answer from public information, rather than disclosing it directly. Legally distinct from disclosure — no classified information changes hands, only the sequence of questions.}
\csdef{hover@Guided deduction}{Teaching someone to discover the answer from public information, rather than disclosing it directly. Legally distinct from disclosure — no classified information changes hands, only the sequence of questions.}
\csdef{hover@topological protection}{Information stored in global patterns is shielded from local disturbances — including heat. This is why topological quantum computers could work at room temperature, when conventional designs require near-absolute-zero cooling.}
\csdef{hover@Topological protection}{Information stored in global patterns is shielded from local disturbances — including heat. This is why topological quantum computers could work at room temperature, when conventional designs require near-absolute-zero cooling.}
\csdef{hover@relinquishment}{Voluntarily surrendering power — not to another owner, but permanently. Not because you're forced to, but because holding it is more dangerous than letting go. The word Bill Joy used in his 2000 essay.}
\csdef{hover@Relinquishment}{Voluntarily surrendering power — not to another owner, but permanently. Not because you're forced to, but because holding it is more dangerous than letting go. The word Bill Joy used in his 2000 essay.}
\csdef{hover@flat worlds}{Real two-dimensional layers inside computer chips and in Earth's magnetic field. Different physics applies there — particles behave in ways impossible in three dimensions. This book treats them as habitats: environments where different rules apply and self-organizing structures can form.}
\csdef{hover@Flat worlds}{Real two-dimensional layers inside computer chips and in Earth's magnetic field. Different physics applies there — particles behave in ways impossible in three dimensions. This book treats them as habitats: environments where different rules apply and self-organizing structures can form.}
\csdef{hover@Special Air Service}{The most elite special forces unit shared by Britain and Australia. Selection has a failure rate above 90 percent. Veterans are bound by the Official Secrets Act for life.}
\csdef{hover@grew}{The system organized itself, the way living things grow. Not engineered component by component — emerged from the physics of the substrate. This is how autocatalytic systems work.}
\csdef{hover@Grew}{The system organized itself, the way living things grow. Not engineered component by component — emerged from the physics of the substrate. This is how autocatalytic systems work.}
\csdef{hover@encryption}{Math that scrambles information so only the intended recipient can read it. Protects passwords, messages, bank accounts, and government secrets. Modern civilization depends on it — break the encryption, break the infrastructure.}
\csdef{hover@Encryption}{Math that scrambles information so only the intended recipient can read it. Protects passwords, messages, bank accounts, and government secrets. Modern civilization depends on it — break the encryption, break the infrastructure.}
\csdef{hover@wormholes}{Real topological connections between distant points in a two-dimensional substrate. Not science fiction — the 2016 Nobel Prize in Physics (Thouless, Haldane, Kosterlitz) was awarded for the theory behind them. Search: topological phases of matter.}
\csdef{hover@Wormholes}{Real topological connections between distant points in a two-dimensional substrate. Not science fiction — the 2016 Nobel Prize in Physics (Thouless, Haldane, Kosterlitz) was awarded for the theory behind them. Search: topological phases of matter.}

% Italic + footnote on first occurrence; italic-only after
\newcommand{\hovertip}[1]{%
  \textit{#1}%
  \ifcsdef{hover@#1}{\footnote{\csuse{hover@#1}}\csundef{hover@#1}}{}%
}
